lens.included_activities: No operating population survives the default lens: this code contains financial legal vehicles and pooled funds, not firms selling recurring outsourced services to external customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Closed-end investment funds, collateralized mortgage obligations, face-amount certificate funds, real estate mortgage investment conduits, special-purpose vehicles, and unit investment trusts are excluded as pooled-asset or financing vehicles. Separate investment advisers, fund managers, administrators, and other fee-based providers are excluded because they are service establishments outside this code.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: The classified vehicles pool or hold investor assets and generate investment or property income for shareholders or unit holders; they do not have a customer service-revenue model. Professional managers and advisers are separate entities.

a.rationale: Wage-weighted task exposure is undefined because no operating outsourced-service firm population remains under the lens, and Census reports that entities in this subsector have little or no employment.
a.change_evidence: A Census establishment microdata tabulation identifying staffed 525990 establishments with recurring external service revenue, plus their occupation mix, would permit estimation.
caveats: Do not import task exposure from investment advisers, administrators, servicers, or portfolio managers that work for these vehicles but are classified elsewhere.

rho.rationale: Five-year implementation cannot be estimated without an eligible operating firm population or defined exposed work inside the code.
rho.change_evidence: Workflow audits from staffed 525990 entities that independently verify external service sales and identify accountable operating processes would establish a basis for this primitive.
caveats: Automation at an external fund adviser or administrator does not establish implementation inside the financial vehicle itself.

e.rationale: Census defines 525990 as legal entities such as closed-end funds, securitization structures, special-purpose vehicles, and unit investment trusts. Its description of subsector 525 states that these entities have no revenue from selling services and that staffed fund-management establishments are classified elsewhere. The eligible share is therefore a structural zero under the outsourced-service-firm lens.
e.change_evidence: Only a revised Census classification or establishment-level evidence that 525990 includes firms with material recurring external service revenue would change this structural boundary.
caveats: The zero applies to eligibility under the frozen lens, not to the vehicles' assets, investment returns, or relevance to capital markets.

s5.rationale: There are no eligible transferable operating firms in the frozen lens, so qualifying five-year control-transfer probability is not defined.
s5.change_evidence: A transaction database identifying control sales of independently operating 525990 service businesses, with NAICS and going-concern verification, would make this estimable.
caveats: Issuance, redemption, secondary trading, liquidation, or transfer of fund interests is not a qualifying control sale of an operating service firm.

q.rationale: Commercial retention is undefined because the classified vehicles do not sell an in-lens recurring service to external customers.
q.change_evidence: Verified contracts and revenue records from correctly classified 525990 firms showing recurring third-party service sales and renewal pricing would provide the required evidence.
caveats: Fund expenses, adviser fees, servicing fees, and shareholder distributions are not evidence of an in-code operator's customer billing structure.

d5.rationale: A five-year ratio for the current service basket is undefined because no qualifying service basket exists inside the frozen lens.
d5.change_evidence: A product-level Census or regulatory dataset demonstrating recurring service output from correctly classified 525990 establishments would define a measurable current basket and forecast base.
caveats: Growth in assets under management, securitization issuance, or fund share demand is not equivalent to demand for an in-scope outsourced service basket.

o.rationale: Operator-required share is undefined because the frozen lens contains no qualifying operator or current service quantity.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of a staffed, accountable 525990 operator selling recurring services, paired with customer workflow research separating software self-service from operator delivery, would permit estimation.
caveats: A vehicle's need for an adviser, trustee, administrator, or servicer does not mean that the provider is classified in 525990.

narrative.executive_view: NAICS 525990 is a residual category for financial legal vehicles, including closed-end funds, securitization structures, and special-purpose vehicles. These are pooled-asset or financing entities rather than outsourced operating-service firms, so none survives the frozen lens.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can affect security analysis, reporting, compliance, administration, and servicing around these vehicles, but that work is performed by advisers and other providers outside the classified vehicle. No defensible in-code task opportunity can be assigned.
narrative.value_capture: The vehicles generate investment or property income and distribute economic results to investors; they do not expose an in-lens customer contract or renewal mechanism through which an operator retains automation benefit.
narrative.firm_availability: The apparent population is composed of funds and legal structures rather than transferable staffed service businesses. Investor.gov confirms that closed-end funds pool investor money while their assets are professionally managed by separately registered investment advisers, reinforcing the vehicle-provider distinction.
narrative.demand_durability: Demand for investment funds, securitizations, and special-purpose structures may continue, but those quantities do not measure a current service basket delivered by a 525990 operator. Service demand belongs to the advisers, administrators, trustees, and servicers around the vehicle.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The central risk is mistaking a large universe of legal vehicles or investment products for a population of operating firms. Some vehicles may have officers, boards, or limited staff, but no evidence found shows recurring external service revenue sufficient to satisfy the lens.
narrative.principal_driver: Census states that subsector 525 vehicles have no service-sale revenue and classifies staffed fund management elsewhere.
narrative.principal_weakness: No eligible operating-service firm population remains under the frozen lens.

sources.title: NAICS Sector 52: Finance and Insurance
sources.supports: Defines 525990 as financial legal entities including closed-end funds, CMOs, REMICs, special-purpose vehicles, and unit investment trusts; states subsector 525 entities earn property income, have little or no employment, and no service-sale revenue; and classifies staffed fund-management establishments elsewhere.

sources.title: Publicly Traded Closed-End Funds
sources.supports: Explains that closed-end funds pool investor money into assets and that their assets are professionally managed by separately registered investment advisers.
